user_query:
Read the product context in input/context.json and the competitor list in input/competitors.csv. Using proven pricing psychology (charm pricing, anchoring, thresholds, decoy, social proof, framing, bundling), design a three-tier MONTHLY pricing structure for the product and write a structured JSON file to output/pricing/recommendations.json that satisfies all constraints below.

Inputs to read:
- input/context.json: product and audience details, including an estimated_monthly_savings_per_customer value to use for ROI framing.
- input/competitors.csv: columns name,monthly_price (numbers). Use this to compute comparison and anchoring.

Output requirements (produce exactly one file at output/pricing/recommendations.json):
- JSON schema (use these exact keys/ids and types):
  {
    "product": string,  
    "tiers": [
      {
        "id": "starter" | "growth" | "premium",
        "name": string,
        "monthly_price": number,                   
        "price_display": string,                   
        "daily_price": number,                     
        "features": string[],                      
        "most_popular": boolean,                   
        "badge": string | null                     
      },
      { ... second tier ... },
      { ... third tier ... }
    ],
    "display_order": ["premium","growth","starter"], 
    "pricing_style": "charm", 
    "decoy_tier_id": "premium", 
    "comparison": {
      "top_competitor_name": string,
      "top_competitor_price": number,
      "growth_savings_vs_top_competitor": number  
    },
    "scarcity": {
      "type": "capacity",
      "limit": 25,
      "applies_to": "growth"
    },
    "bundling": {
      "growth": {
        "components": [ { "name": string, "implied_value": number } ],
        "itemized_value_total": number
      }
    },
    "framing": {
      "growth": {
        "roi": {
          "estimated_monthly_savings": number,          
          "days_to_payback": number                     
        }
      }
    }
  }

Hard constraints to satisfy in your JSON:
1) Exactly three tiers with ids starter, growth, premium.
2) All monthly_price values must end in .99 (charm pricing) and be in USD-equivalent numbers (no currency symbols in the number; include the symbol only in price_display).
3) Thresholds:
   - starter: monthly_price <= 29.99
   - growth: monthly_price <= 99.99
   - premium: 110.00 <= monthly_price <= 199.99
4) Relative price ratios:
   - growth / starter must be between 1.6 and 2.2 (inclusive)
   - premium / growth must be between 1.1 and 1.3 (inclusive) to act as a close-price decoy
5) Anchoring & order:
   - display_order must be ["premium","growth","starter"] (highest price first)
6) Social proof:
   - growth must have most_popular=true and badge="Most Popular"; other tiers must have most_popular=false and badge=null.
7) Decoy:
   - decoy_tier_id must be "premium".
8) Framing math:
   - daily_price for each tier = round(monthly_price / 30, 2)
   - framing.growth.roi.estimated_monthly_savings must equal input/context.json's estimated_monthly_savings_per_customer
   - framing.growth.roi.days_to_payback = ceil(growth monthly_price / (estimated_monthly_savings_per_customer / 30))
9) Comparison using competitors:
   - top_competitor_price = the maximum monthly_price in input/competitors.csv
   - growth_savings_vs_top_competitor = round(top_competitor_price - growth monthly_price, 2) (non-negative)
10) Bundling/unbundling for growth tier:
   - Provide at least three components
   - itemized_value_total = sum of component implied_value numbers
   - itemized_value_total must be >= growth monthly_price * 1.2
11) Features:
   - Each tier must include at least three features.

Notes:
- Use ethical scarcity: capacity-based only, exactly as specified in the schema above.
- Derive any comparisons and ROI strictly from the provided inputs and your chosen prices.
- Do not produce any additional files beyond output/pricing/recommendations.json.
